summary: Frontend-only E2E test of Neuroforge iteration 2. All 4 new features PASS end-to-end: (1) Task Switching launcher + runner + switch-cost summary, (2) OSPAN launcher + memorize→distractor(arithmetic)→recall + memory-vs-distractor breakdown summary, (3) PASAT rolling window (window=3) with updated header/hint and per-op accuracy on last W-1 valid trials, (4) Daily Delayed Recall dashboard card in both memorize and recall (backdated) modes, incl. skip, config, streak. Registry integration confirmed (Analytics + Sessions task filter list Task Switching & Operation Span). Persistence across page reloads confirmed for both task sessions and daily-recall state. No new critical bugs.

issues: Chart still labelled 'ROLLING ACCURACY · WINDOW=5' regardless of task's configured windowSize (=3 in test). Confusing wording since 'window' now means two different things in PASAT (rolling accuracy chart window vs. PASAT op window). Consider renaming the chart label to 'ROLLING ACCURACY · CHART WINDOW=5' or make the stat-chart window configurable per task.

action_items: Optional: rename SessionSummary's 'ROLLING ACCURACY · WINDOW=5' label to something like 'ROLLING ACCURACY · SMOOTHING=5' to avoid semantic collision with new PASAT windowSize config.
critical_code_review_comments: DailyChallenge.jsx line 39: submitRecall() is called without a config arg — this replaces state.cfg with default merge because submitRecall's `cfg` param merges into next challenge. Currently harmless because DEFAULT_RECALL_CFG shape matches state.cfg, but user-customized cfg persists only because state.cfg is spread first. This works but is fragile — passing `state.cfg` explicitly would be safer.

OSPAN Runner: recall input uses per-item single-character parsing for 'letters' (line 110 `.split('')`), which means letters typed as space/comma-separated like 'A B C' get their spaces stripped and treated as 'ABC' correctly, but numbers input like '1 2 3' correctly uses regex — good. However for 'mixed' item type where letters and words mix in same trial, users may not know how to enter items — no on-screen hint about separator format.

TaskSwitching Runner: `commitNoResponse` and `respond` both check trial idx, but if user clicks BEFORE stimulusMs elapses on a re-render (React StrictMode double-effect), the trial loop useEffect could fire twice; guarded by responsesRef.find(index===trialIdx) which prevents duplicate response entries — GOOD.

PASAT reduceWindow: for 'avg' opKey computes overall mean of the window (documented in comments), but the `expected` reduces via `Math.round(val)` when cfg.decimals=false — rounding a 3-value average may hide small errors. Consider allowing tolerance ±1 during answer scoring.

dailyRecall.seedFromDate uses `Date.now() % 100` as jitter (line 33) — this means the challenge changes each time ensureTodaysChallenge is called during the same day. The 'pending' object guards against regeneration (line 86 early-return), so in practice this only matters for the initial seed. OK.
TaskSwitchingRunner clears timers on effect cleanup, but audio (beep) is not stopped if user hits exit mid-session — same as flagged in iteration 1 for NBack/PASAT.
seed_data_creation: For recall-mode simulation, backdated localStorage 'neuroforge:v1:dailyRecall'.pending.generatedOn to '2020-01-01' via page.evaluate then reloaded (as per review request).
context_for_next_testing_agent: All iteration 2 features work as spec'd. Data-testids used: task-card-{id}, ts-settings, ts-cfg-quadrant-0..3, ts-cfg-rounds, ts-cfg-switch-prob, ts-respond-A/B, ts-quadrant-0..3, ts-stimulus, ts-exit-btn, ts-switch-cost, ospan-settings, ospan-cfg-item/recall/distractor-arithmetic/distractor-spatial/start/max, ospan-runner, ospan-item, ospan-distractor-prompt, ospan-distractor-yes/no/same/different, ospan-recall-input, ospan-recall-submit, ospan-breakdown, pasat-cfg-window, daily-recall-card, memorize-mode/phrase/number, new-challenge-btn, recall-config-toggle, cfg-word-count/number-length/charset, recall-cfg-save, recall-mode, recall-phrase-input, recall-number-input, recall-submit-btn, recall-skip-btn, recall-result, recall-streak, recall-longest, analytics-task-filter. When testing OSPAN runners, note distractor sometimes appears before recall — script must handle both arithmetic (yes/no) and spatial (same/different) even if only 'arithmetic' toggle is enabled by default. When testing PASAT window=3, first 2 trials have no expected answer (correct — only W-1..N-1 accept responses). Sessions persist to localStorage key 'neuroforge:v1:sessions'; each fresh mcp_browser_automation call = fresh browser context = fresh localStorage.
rca of the issue: N/A — no bugs found in iteration 2. Iteration 1's restoreSession StrictMode bug was verified fixed in a previous run per handoff note.
